rulings.Case Title: ARKADY BERGER ET AL VS. DAVID DEDIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Wednesday, March 25, 2026, Line 1. PLAINTIFF ARKADY BERGER, and NELLY MERZHEITSKY's MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. Continued to April 23, 2026, to be heard after the motion to withdraw deemed admissions is adjudicated. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: RICHAELE AFFANNATO VS. MAXIMUS REAL ESTATE PARTNERS, LTD., A CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories General To Defendant Maximus Real Estate Partners, Ltd.
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Wednesday, March 25, 2026, Line 2. 2 - Plaintiff Richaele Affannato's Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories General Law (Set Two) To Defendant Maximus Real Estate Partners, Ltd is OFF CALENDAR. Plaintiff has not demonstrated their moving papers were timely served. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 1005(b) [motion must be filed and served 16 court days before hearing]; Code of Civil Procedure section 1013(a) [service by mail where place of address and place of mailing are within State of adds 5 calendar days to the period of notice]; see Code of Civil Procedure section 1010.6(a)(3)(B) [service by email adds two court days to "any period of notice"].) Plaintiff served their moving papers on Defendants electronically on 3/2/2026, which is not 16 court days plus two court days before 3/25/2026, the hearing date noticed. The court notes it is not inclined to expend scarce resources adjudicating merits discovery motions in settled cases, nor do parties have a right to such adjudication. Prior to the hearing, Plaintiff shall lodge by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org a proposed order repeating the above verbatim. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: JUSTIN ESGUERRA VS. ROBERT STENSON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Robert Stenson'S Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set One, Pursuant To Ccp 2030.300 Et. Seq.; Request For Costs, Sanctions, And Attorney'S Fees Under Ccp 2023.010 Et Seq. Against Defendant Robert Stenson And/Or His Counsel Of Record, Toschi Doyle Houvener Wellen, In The Amount Of $2,310.00; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Angelica Jimenez In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Wednesday, March 25, 2026, Line 3. 3 - Plaintiff Justin Esguerra's Motion To Compel Defendant Robert Stenson's Further Responses To Request for Production of Documents, Set One, Pursuant To CCP 2031.310 Et. Seq.; Request For Sanctions Against Defendant and Counsel In The Amount Of $2,310.00 is DENIED. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties). For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)
